enonce.texte: Transform the following yes/no questions into reported speech. Use if or whether.

questions.texte: Change these direct questions into reported questions.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked me: "Are you a student?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked me if I was a student.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Do you speak English?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked if I spoke English.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher asked: "Have you finished your homework?"
sousQuestions.reponse: The teacher asked if we had finished our homework.

sousQuestions.texte: My mother asked: "Are you hungry?"
sousQuestions.reponse: My mother asked if I was hungry.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "Did you see the movie?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked if I had seen the movie.

enonce.texte: Transform the following wh-questions into reported speech. Maintain the question word (who, what, where, when, why, how).

questions.texte: Change these direct questions into reported questions.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Where do you live?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked where I lived.

sousQuestions.texte: The police officer asked: "What are you doing here?"
sousQuestions.reponse: The police officer asked what I was doing there.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "Why did you leave early?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked why I had left early.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher asked: "Who wrote this poem?"
sousQuestions.reponse: The teacher asked who had written that poem.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "When will you arrive?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked when I would arrive.

enonce.texte: Transform the following mixed questions into reported speech. Pay attention to tense backshift and pronoun changes.

questions.texte: Change these direct questions into reported questions.

sousQuestions.texte: My friend asked: "Can you help me with this exercise?"
sousQuestions.reponse: My friend asked if I could help him with that exercise.

sousQuestions.texte: The journalist asked: "How did you feel about the results?"
sousQuestions.reponse: The journalist asked how I had felt about the results.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Have you ever been to London?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked if I had ever been to London.

sousQuestions.texte: The manager asked: "Why haven't you finished the report?"
sousQuestions.reponse: The manager asked why I hadn't finished the report.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "Are you going to the meeting tomorrow?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked if I was going to the meeting the next day.

enonce.texte: Transform these questions into reported speech. Pay special attention to subject-verb order (no inversion in reported questions).

questions.texte: Change these direct questions into reported questions.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "What is the matter with you?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked what the matter was with me.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "Where has John gone?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked where John had gone.

sousQuestions.texte: The doctor asked: "How are you feeling today?"
sousQuestions.reponse: The doctor asked how I was feeling that day.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Which book did you choose?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked which book I had chosen.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "What time does the train leave?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked what time the train left.

enonce.texte: You are an HR manager who has just conducted a job interview. Report what you asked the candidate.

questions.texte: Report the questions you asked during the interview.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "What is your name?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked the candidate what his/her name was.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "Why do you want this job?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked why he/she wanted that job.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "Can you work under pressure?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked if he/she could work under pressure.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "How many years of experience do you have?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked how many years of experience he/she had.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "When can you start?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked when he/she could start.

enonce.texte: You are a police officer questioning a witness. Report the questions you asked to your colleague.

questions.texte: Report the questions you asked the witness.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "Where were you on the night of the crime?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked the witness where he/she had been on the night of the crime.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "Did you see anything suspicious?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked if he/she had seen anything suspicious.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "What did the suspect look like?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked what the suspect had looked like.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "Can you describe the car?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked if he/she could describe the car.

sousQuestions.texte: I asked: "Why didn't you call the police immediately?"
sousQuestions.reponse: I asked why he/she hadn't called the police immediately.

enonce.texte: Transform these questions into reported speech. Remember to change pronouns and time/place expressions.

questions.texte: Change these direct questions into reported questions.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Are you going to the party tonight?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked if I was going to the party that night.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "Did you see her here yesterday?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked if I had seen her there the day before.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Will you be here next week?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked if I would be there the following week.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked: "How long have you been waiting for me?"
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked how long I had been waiting for him.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked: "Where are you going tomorrow?"
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked where I was going the next day.

enonce.texte: You are a journalist who has just interviewed the Minister of Education. Write a news report reporting the questions you asked.

questions.texte: Write a news report with at least 6 reported questions.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse:

During an exclusive interview, I had the opportunity to speak with the Minister of Education. I asked the Minister what the government's plan was to improve the quality of education. She replied that significant reforms were underway. I then asked why some schools still lacked basic facilities. She explained that budget constraints were a major challenge. I inquired whether the government would increase teachers' salaries. She said that this was under consideration. I also asked when the new curriculum would be implemented. She answered that it would be launched next year. Furthermore, I asked how the government would address the issue of school dropouts. She outlined several strategies, including awareness campaigns and scholarship programs. Finally, I asked if the Minister was confident about the future of education. She expressed her optimism and commitment to the cause.
